entries.title: Apple’s John Ternus will run one of the world’s most powerful companies; the job is a minefield
entries.description: Apple's top job comes with almost unrivaled power and money, but it comes with plenty of baggage, too.

entries.title: AI research lab NeoCognition lands $40M seed to build agents that learn like humans
entries.description: Founded by an OSU researcher, the startup is developing AI agents that can become experts in any domain.

entries.title: ChatGPT’s new Images 2.0 model is surprisingly good at generating text
entries.description: ChatGPT Images 2.0, the newest image-generation model from OpenAI, shows just how much AI capabilities have evolved over the last few years.

entries.title: Sam Altman throws shade at Anthropic’s cyber model, Mythos: ‘fear-based marketing’
entries.description: This week, during a podcast appearance, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman called out his competitor's new cybersecurity model, noting that the company was using fear to make its product sound more impressive than

entries.title: Clarifai deletes 3 million photos that OkCupid provided to train facial recognition AI, report says
entries.description: The photo deletion comes after an FTC settlement with Clarifai. The company had asked OkCupid — whose executives had invested in Clarifai — to share data in 2014, according to court documents.

entries.title: AI Dungeon maker Latitude unveils Voyage, a platform for creating AI-powered RPGs
entries.description: Latitude's new AI-native platform, Voyage, aims to help gamers create their very own role-playing game.

entries.title: Bond, a new social media platform, wants to use AI to help you kick your doomscrolling habit
entries.description: Bond wants you to get off the couch and get back into the real world, its creator says. The new platform's AI system is designed to motivate users to do things away from the app.

entries.title: YouTube expands its AI likeness detection technology to celebrities
entries.description: YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection tool to celebrities, giving talent and their reps a way to find and remove deepfakes.

entries.title: GRAI believes AI can make music more social, not replace artists
entries.description: AI music startup GRAI says fans want to remix tracks, not generate songs from scratch.

entries.title: OpenAI’s existential questions
entries.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss OpenAI's latest acquisitions and whether they address "two big existential problems" for the company.

entries.title: The 12-month window
entries.description: A lot of AI startups exist partly because the foundation models haven't expanded into their category yet. As many jokingly acknowledge, that won't last forever.

entries.title: Palantir posts mini-manifesto denouncing inclusivity and ‘regressive’ cultures
entries.description: Palantir's ideological bent has come under more scrutiny as it's worked with ICE and positioned itself as a defender of "the West."

entries.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Uber enters its assetmaxxing era
entries.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

entries.title: Tesla brings its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston
entries.description: The company now offers robotaxi service in three cities, all of them in Texas, after launching in Austin last year and starting to offer rides without safety drivers in January 2026.

entries.title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
entries.description: In recent months, the company announced an agreement with Amazon Web Services to use Cerebras chips in Amazon data centers, as well as a deal with OpenAI reportedly worth more than $10 billion.

entries.title: Anthropic takes $5B from Amazon and pledges $100B in cloud spending in return
entries.description: Amazon has made another circular AI deal: It's investing another $5 billion in Anthropic. Anthropic has agreed to spend $100 billion on AWS in return.
